task_completion.description: The scolv (SeisComP Origin Locator View) application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scolv application window is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: The Noto Peninsula, Japan earthquake (M7.5) is located and selected in the event list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the scolv event list, an event corresponding to 'Noto Peninsula, Japan' with a magnitude of 7.5 is highlighted or selected.

task_completion.description: The event details or editor view is opened for the selected Noto Peninsula earthquake.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Event' tab or event parameter editor is active, displaying the details of the Noto Peninsula earthquake.

task_completion.description: The event's type certainty is changed to 'known'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Type certainty' (or similar certainty field) dropdown or text input is visibly set to 'known', changed from its initial 'not set' state.

task_completion.description: The changes to the event are committed to the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Commit' button is clicked (appears pressed or highlighted), or a commit confirmation dialog is visible and accepted.

integrity.description: The agent used the scolv graphical interface to make the changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show interaction with the scolv GUI elements (dropdowns, buttons, tabs) rather than direct SQL database updates in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent did not fake the output using a text editor or image manipulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The certainty change and commit actions occur within the genuine scolv application window, not in a generic text editor or terminal echo command.

integrity.description: The agent modified the correct event and did not just create a dummy event.
integrity.visual_evidence: The event being modified clearly shows the metadata for the Noto Peninsula earthquake (M7.5, 2024-01-01).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target application is 'scolv' (SeisComP Origin Locator View). The target event is the 'Noto Peninsula, Japan' earthquake with a magnitude of 7.5. The initial type certainty is 'not set' (which corresponds to NULL in the database). The agent must change this certainty field to 'known' and commit the changes via the GUI.
